data.title: Barton Crockett Reiterates Buy on Comcast, Sees Underappreciated Media Growth and Strategic Upside Supporting $31 Price Target
data.description:
Rosenblatt Securities analyst Barton Crockett has maintained their bullish stance on CMCSA stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Barton Crockett has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Comcast’s solid fundamentals and underappreciated strategic positioning. Despite a mixed second-quarter print and a sharp one-day share decline driven by broader market weakness, Comcast modestly exceeded expectations on revenue and earnings, while key subscriber trends largely matched forecasts, suggesting operational stability rather than deterioration.

He also points to improving growth drivers in media and streaming, highlighted by strong advertising momentum and Peacock’s move into profitability, alongside a robust film slate and continued box-office leadership at Universal. Crockett believes the market is assigning low expectations to these assets, with Comcast trading at a modest enterprise-value-to-EBITDA multiple and supported by a sum-of-the-parts valuation, which together underpin his $31 price target and support a constructive view on potential upside and future strategic or merger appeal.

In another report released yesterday, Bank of America Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $37.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Hims & Hers Health: Regulatory Tailwinds and Peptide Opportunity Support Buy Rating, $40 Price Target Reiterated
data.description:
Analyst Maria Ripps of Canaccord Genuity maintained a Buy rating on Hims & Hers Health, retaining the price target of $40.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Maria Ripps has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that strengthen Hims & Hers Health’s long-term growth outlook. She views the Pharmacy Compounding Advisory Committee’s unexpected support for allowing peptides like BPC-157 and KPV to be compounded as a clear positive, reinforcing her thesis that regulatory reclassification can open a large new addressable market for the company. This early regulatory momentum, combined with Hims’s strong stock performance around the vote, supports maintaining a constructive stance and an unchanged $40 price target.

Ripps also highlights that, while formal FDA rulemaking will take time and any earnings contribution is unlikely before 2027, Hims is well positioned to capture future demand. The firm’s domestic manufacturing assets acquired from CSBio, its established 503A compounding infrastructure, and in-house testing capabilities are seen as key competitive advantages in winning share of an estimated $13B–$26B peptide market. Given these structural strengths and the favorable direction of early regulatory signals, she believes the risk/reward justifies a continued Buy rating despite the long lead time to monetization.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $39.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Ryan Nash Reiterates Hold Rating with Unchanged $2340 Price Target Amid Solid Execution but Ongoing Uncertainties
data.description:
In a report released yesterday, Ryan Nash from Goldman Sachs maintained a Hold rating on First Citizens BancShares, with a price target of $2,340.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ryan Nash has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors reflecting both solid execution and lingering uncertainties. The latest quarter showed stronger-than-expected pre-provision earnings, supported by higher loan yields and robust fee income, while credit quality surprised positively with lower net charge-offs and stable non-performing loans.

At the same time, balance sheet momentum was mixed, with loan growth concentrated in SVB-related exposures and deposit gains driven mainly by the direct bank, and management’s updated 2026 outlook, while modestly improved, still hinges heavily on the path of net interest income and deposit costs in a competitive funding environment. Nash also notes that questions remain around the timing and impact of repaying the FDIC note and future capital return, leading him to keep a neutral stance with an unchanged $2340 price target rather than move to a more decisive Buy or Sell recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Nash is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 11.8% and a 67.83% success rate. Nash covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Bread Financial Holdings, First Hawaiian, and First Citizens BancShares.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $2,346.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Digital Realty: Strong Results and Raised Guidance, But Rich Valuation Keeps Rating at Hold
data.description:
Digital Realty, the Real Estate sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Michael Funk from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Hold rating on the stock and has a $215.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Michael Funk has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including strong recent performance that appears largely reflected in the share price. Digital Realty delivered better‑than‑expected rental revenue, EBITDA and core FFO, supported by robust bookings and materially higher rental rate spreads on renewals, and management raised guidance to reflect this improved outlook.

At the same time, Funk notes that the stock already trades at a rich valuation multiple versus the broader REIT sector, even if it is modestly below the company’s historical peak and slightly behind its closest peer. In his view, Digital Realty remains a high‑quality operator with solid multi‑year growth visibility, but the current valuation appropriately discounts these secular tailwinds, limiting near‑term upside and justifying a Neutral, or Hold, stance.

In another report released today, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $197.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan: Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Strong 2Q26 Execution Supports Unchanged $74 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Nick Cash from Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy rating on Freeport-McMoRan and keeping the price target at $74.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Nick Cash has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Freeport-McMoRan’s stronger-than-expected 2Q26 performance and the continued successful ramp-up at Grasberg. He highlights that management reaffirmed 2026 Grasberg copper and gold output guidance while trimming unit cash costs, signaling solid operational execution despite some near-term uncertainties.

He also underscores incremental upside from higher projected molybdenum sales and the company’s disciplined capital allocation, even as capex rises modestly for projects like Bagdad and Grasberg’s energy transition. Using a blended valuation that combines a through-cycle EV/EBITDA framework with a strategic M&A component, he maintains a $74 price target, which offers sufficient upside relative to current trading levels to justify a Buy rating, while acknowledging commodity and operational risks.

In another report released today, BMO Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $78.00 price target.

FCX’s price has also changed slightly for the past six months – from $60.410 to $63.500, which is a 5.12% increase.
